rationale: The citing paper uses the cited study as direct prior evidence that moment-to-moment affective content influences zapping, explicitly invoking it when developing hypotheses about retention effects and when motivating inclusion of emotion level and velocity terms. It also cites that work in setting up controls related to ad-viewing avoidance research, not just in a generic literature list. This indicates the cited paper materially informs the citing paper's conceptual framing and modeling choices. The 2012 study then moves this line of work to internet video ads and specific emotions with new measurement/modeling elements.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on the cited paper's moment-to-moment ad-avoidance findings to motivate its own dynamic hypotheses and model of viewing retention. It extends that prior TV-commercial work to online video advertising by focusing on specific emotions (joy, surprise) and attention-mediated effects.
